# blaze-motion

> A tuned motion engine and a set of composable, RSC-safe animation primitives for Next.js + shadcn, curated on top of the motion.dev (`motion`) library — a six-primitive core plus entrance, hover, stagger, and text/number effects. Installed the shadcn way — the source files land in your repo, editable — not as an opaque npm dependency. The whole engine's feel is retuned by editing one `feel` object.

## Install

- URL (works today, zero setup — no components.json entry required): `npx shadcn add https://motion.asmitsah.dev/r/all.json`
- Single runtime dependency: `motion@^12.42.2`
- One command installs every file (lib/motion.ts + the provider + all primitives and effects) — the single "all" registry item.

## The feel object (lib/motion.ts)

One block tunes the whole engine: `duration` (fast 0.2 / base 0.55 / slow 0.8), `ease` [0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1], `easeSoft` [0.4, 0, 0.2, 1] (page transitions only), `rise` 28px, `inView` 0.3, `stagger` 0.08. Per-instance props still override.

## The core six primitives

- MotionProvider — mounts once in the root layout; wraps children in LazyMotion (domAnimation, strict) + MotionConfig (reducedMotion="user"). Strict mode requires importing `m` from `motion/react-m`.
- FadeIn — pure opacity fade for above-the-fold / on-mount content (no scroll trigger).
- Reveal — scroll-triggered straight rise from below (opacity 0→1, y +28→0), once, at 30% in view. No spring, no scale.
- Stagger / StaggerItem — two flat named exports (never a compound Stagger.Item, which is undefined across the RSC client-reference boundary and crashes next build's prerender). Container staggers children; item does the fadeUp rise.
- CinematicImage — scale-down settle (opacity 0 / scale 1.1 → opacity 1 / scale 1) for large images only; the wrapper must be overflow-hidden.
- PageTransition — wraps route content in AnimatePresence (mode="wait"); fades + shifts on route change with easeSoft. Keyed via a `routeKey` prop, not `key`.

## Entrance / hover / stagger / text effects

- SpringPop — scale-up overshoot spring (0.8 → ~1.05 → 1) + opacity on mount; the one deliberate overshoot, never on titles.
- BlurToFocus — filter blur(10px) → blur(0) + opacity on mount; GPU-costly filter, hero / single focal element only.
- LineDraw + LineDrawPath — SVG line-draw; each path animates pathLength 0→1 then fades its fill in. Two flat exports; LineDraw IS the <svg>.
- AnimatedUnderline — underline draws L→R on hover, retracts out on leave; pure CSS, polymorphic via `as` (pass Link for internal nav).
- SiblingDimming + SiblingDimmingItem — hover one grid card, siblings dim to 50% via :has(); no JS. Two flat exports.
- RadialStagger — click a grid tile and a spring-pop ripples outward, each cell delayed by distance from the clicked origin.
- TextReveal — per-word or per-char staggered straight rise (no scale); full string on aria-label for screen readers.
- NumberTicker — count-up to a target on scroll-into-view, tabular-nums so width never jitters; reduced motion snaps to the value.
- ScrollProgressBar — fixed, spring-smoothed bar whose scaleX tracks page (or a target element's) scroll; aria-hidden.
- Stagger's linear step is tunable via staggerChildren / delayChildren props (backward-compatible defaults 0.08 / 0.05).

## Design principles

- RSC-safe: every primitive is a 'use client' leaf (a few pure-CSS effects are Server Components); pages composed around them stay Server Components. Never a compound X.Item — flat named exports only, or next build's prerender crashes.
- className / style passthrough on every primitive, so the primitive itself is the layout element.
- reducedMotion="user" is honored automatically — motion-driven effects drop transforms to an opacity/instant state; CSS effects carry motion-reduce:transition-none.

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/blaze-000/blaze-motion
- Site: https://motion.asmitsah.dev
- Foundation: https://motion.dev
